label: Data as raw, unprocessed material
description: Conceives of data as raw, unprocessed, or in its base state before treatment. Includes explicit mentions of 'raw' or 'unprocessed'. Out: mentions of processing that focus on the resulting insight rather than the raw starting point.
exemplars: Unprocessed information; Data is a collection of raw facts, information and figures.; Data is a collection of raw facts, figures, observations, or measurements

label: Data as facts, figures and observations
description: Defines data by its constituent elements: facts, numbers, figures, words, observations, measurements, images, dates. In: any enumeration of concrete component types. Out: definitions that only say 'information' without listing constituent facts/figures.
exemplars: Data is a collection of facts, numbers, words, observations or other useful information.; Data are facts, figures, observations, or information collected for analysis; These can include numbers, text, images, dates, or other recorded information

label: Data as a collection of information
description: Frames data as something gathered or collected together, emphasising the act or result of collection/aggregation. In: 'collection of', 'collected', 'gathered from various sources'. Out: definitions with no notion of gathering.
exemplars: A collection of information collected.; Collection of facts/information; gathered from various sources

label: Data equated with information
description: Treats data and information as the same thing, defining data straightforwardly as information. In: 'data is information' with no distinction drawn. Out: definitions that separate raw data from processed information.
exemplars: Data is information whether it be organised or unorganised; Data is information..evidence used from various sources; Unprocessed information

label: Data as a basis for decisions and action
description: Defines data by its purpose: enabling, guiding, supporting or improving decision-making, actions, or business outcomes. In: any reference to decisions, actions, insights that improve outcomes. Out: definitions purely about what data is made of.
exemplars: Data is anything collected that enables you to make better decision or leads you to take actions.; It help to take decision; used to support better decision-making

label: Data becomes meaningful once processed
description: Conceives of data as meaningless or limited on its own until organised, processed or analysed, at which point it becomes meaningful information, insight or knowledge. In: an explicit transformation from raw data to insight/meaning. Out: definitions that treat data as already meaningful information.
exemplars: Through data processing and data analysis, organizations transform raw data points into valuable insights; on their own, may not have much meaning... When data is organized, processed, and analyzed, it becomes meaningful information; Data is information which is generated, evaluated and understood which guides decision making

label: Data as foundation or lifeblood
description: Frames data as a foundational prerequisite or lifeblood underpinning systems, insight and knowledge. In: metaphors of foundation, lifeblood, prerequisite, source from which value flows. Out: functional decision-support framing without the foundational metaphor.
exemplars: Data is the lifeblood of any information system. It is the prerequisite for effective system building and interaction.; data is the foundation from which valuable insights and knowledge are created

label: Data as lifecycle of activities
description: Defines data in terms of the activities performed on it — collection, analysis, storage, reporting — rather than as a static object. In: enumerations of process steps as part of the definition. Out: single-step mentions of processing aimed only at meaning.
exemplars: Collection, analysis and storage of series of information on a particular topic or subject for reporting and enable decision making; collected for analysis and used to support better decision-making
